mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points within a single month signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline by at least 15% in out-of-sample Sharpe ratio, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of supply-side bottlenecks, a sustained decline in the industrial production index signals capacity constraints that compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO declines by > 1.5% month-over-month, or the hypothesis yields a negative predictive value below 0.40 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP exceeding the long-term trend rate of 2.0% annually signals robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window, or the proportion of positive 5-day returns following GDP beats falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5%, the cost of capital exceeds the historical equilibrium for corporate debt servicing, triggering margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for > 3 consecutive months despite FEDFUNDS remaining above 4.5%, or the threshold crossing event shows no statistically significant difference in forward returns compared to sub-4.5% periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth deceleration and industrial production contraction indicate a synchronized demand-supply mismatch that precedes earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal does not improve out-of-sample classification accuracy by at least 5% over a logistic regression baseline using single series, or the interaction coefficient fails to reject the null hypothesis of zero effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a month-over-month increase in housing starts acts as a leading indicator for construction and durable goods spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to generate positive SPX returns in > 50% of instances during identified recovery periods, or the forward return differential between rising and falling HOUST months is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
